title: CI green: windows.yml split suites, session-env hermeticity, deterministic claim_race barrier, root-skip chmod
action: (1) .github/workflows/windows.yml: remove the 'bee-hive template test_lib' step (test_lib.mjs was deleted in the suite split); replace with steps running the actual split template suites (the suites under skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/ that are portable to Windows — mirror what scripts/run_verify.mjs discovers, or invoke a runner subset). Keep portable-paths guard and config validate steps runnable. (2) scripts/run_verify.mjs: scrub CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and BEE_SESSION_ID from every child suite env so local runs match CI. (3) skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_state.mjs AND test_claims.mjs: delete those two env vars at bootstrap (defense in depth). Confirm test_state passes hermetically: the lane-overlap check at test_state.mjs:546-568 must now exercise the real sessionless path — if the SESSION_REQUIRED refusal makes the scenario impossible, give the reserve call an explicit session (sess-them) so the hold really exists; the test intent is overlap refusal, not sessionless reserve. (4) scripts/test_claim_race.mjs (g-unsafe) negative control: replace sleep-based ordering with a deterministic barrier (fs-based ready files or a pipe handshake between racers) so the stale-snapshot revert is demonstrated 10/10. (5) Find the chmod-0555-based write-failure simulation (review says test_cells) and skip it loudly when process.geteuid?.()===0.

truths: node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs exits 0 on 10 consecutive runs
windows.yml contains no reference to test_lib.mjs and every suite path it invokes exists in the repo
prohibitions: Do not weaken the SESSION_REQUIRED guard itself (that is cell 1710-5 surface)
Do not delete the negative control — make it deterministic

verify: env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_state.mjs && env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_claims.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs

trace.outcome: CI green: windows.yml drops the deleted test_lib.mjs step and instead runs all skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/ suites via a new BEE_VERIFY_ROOT_FILTER env var on run_verify.mjs (reuses its own discovery, restricted to one root -- zero hand-maintained list). run_verify.mjs now scrubs CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID/BEE_SESSION_ID from every spawned child so local runs match CI; test_state.mjs and test_claims.mjs delete both vars at bootstrap too (defense in depth). Fixed the one hermetic-only failure this surfaced: test_state.mjs's lane-overlap reservation check was silently relying on an inherited session env to bypass SESSION_REQUIRED -- now passes an explicit session and asserts the reservation actually landed. test_claim_race.mjs's (g-unsafe) negative control replaced its 50ms sleep-based race ordering with a deterministic fs-based ready-file barrier between the racer processes (reads-before-claim, writes-after-claim), demonstrating the stale-snapshot revert 10/10 instead of ~7/10. test_cells.mjs's chmod-0555 write-failure simulation now detects process.geteuid?.()===0 and skips loudly (root bypasses chmod, so the simulated failure could never fire there) instead of risking a false red on root-run CI.
trace.verify_output: RED (pre-fix, captured before edits): hermetic test_state.mjs failed 1/31 at the lane-overlap reservation check (sessionless reserve() hit SESSION_REQUIRED under two live sessions, so no hold was ever created) -- "expected an error, none thrown". test_claim_race.mjs failed 3/10 consecutive runs with "(g-unsafe) DETECTOR DID NOT BITE: final status is claimed" (sleep-based ordering was not reliably ordered). GREEN (post-fix): env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_state.mjs -> 31 passed, 0 failed, exit 0. env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_claims.mjs -> 21 passed, 0 failed, exit 0. node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs x10 consecutive (5 with harness env present, 5 hermetic) -> exit 0 every time, "(g-unsafe)" scenario now uses a deterministic fs-based ready-file barrier instead of a sleep. node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cells.mjs -> 92 passed, 0 failed (chmod-0555 write-failure simulation now skips loudly under a simulated root euid, confirmed via a geteuid monkeypatch smoke test: prints "SKIP resetCellBudget audit-order chmod(0o555)..." and still passes). windows.yml: grep for test_lib confirms zero references (only removed from the deleted-step comment), YAML parses cleanly via python3 yaml.safe_load, and BEE_VERIFY_ROOT_FILTER=skills/bee-hive/templates/tests node scripts/run_verify.mjs discovers and runs all 15 template suites cross-platform-safely via run_verify.mjs own discovery (only pre-existing failure there is test_state.mjs lane-overlap, now fixed). Full node scripts/run_verify.mjs shows one unrelated pre-existing failure (skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_plugin_distribution.mjs release-manifest sha256 drift) caused by sibling in-flight cells (1710-2/1710-6) touching lock.mjs/hooks/catalog.mjs ahead of the 1710-9 close-out render+manifest step -- outside this cell scope, none of my 6 files affect plugin skill tree rendering.
trace.verify_command: env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_state.mjs && env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_claims.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs
